skill.name: performance-gate
skill.description: Review code changes for obvious performance risks, including inefficient queries, missing pagination, render churn, and memory leaks.
skill.summary: Run a lightweight performance review gate before code moves forward.
skill.author: DanielPodolsky

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution and a weak cryptographic algorithm, but review found no executable scripts or cryptographic code. The reported locations are Markdown fences, inline examples, and descriptive text in SKILL.md, so the findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code blocks and inline examples used to show performance review output and application code. No evidence found of executable Ruby backticks, shell commands, or command invocation logic in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown examples and inline formatting, not runnable skill code. The file contains only review instructions and no script entry point.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the skill description line. No evidence found of cryptographic functions, hashing algorithms, encryption code, or credential handling in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is YAML front matter describing the performance gate. It does not contain cryptographic code or algorithm names used in a security-sensitive context.

content.value_statement: Performance issues often appear only after data volume or traffic grows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a focused review for common scalability risks.
actual_capabilities: Checks database access patterns for N+1 queries, missing pagination, broad selects, and missing indexes.
Reviews frontend code for expensive render calculations, avoidable re-renders, and missing memoization.
Flags API and network patterns that return oversized payloads or make redundant calls.
Asks scale-focused review questions about 10,000 or 1,000,000 item scenarios.
Produces pass or warning review outcomes with concise explanations and locations.
limitations: Does not run benchmarks, profilers, load tests, or production telemetry analysis.
Focuses on obvious performance risks rather than micro-optimizations.
May miss framework-specific bottlenecks that require runtime traces.
Returns warnings, not hard release-blocking decisions.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Performance Check
use_cases.description: Review a feature branch for common performance regressions before requesting approval.

use_cases.title: Frontend Render Review
use_cases.description: Check React components for repeated sorting, unstable callbacks, large lists, and unnecessary re-renders.

use_cases.title: API Scalability Review
use_cases.description: Inspect endpoints for N+1 queries, missing pagination, excessive payloads, and inefficient data access.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Performance Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-gate skill to review this change. Tell me whether it passes or has warnings, and focus on obvious performance issues.

prompt_templates.title: Database Query Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the performance-gate skill to this backend change. Check for N+1 queries, missing pagination, inefficient selects, and missing indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Frontend Rendering Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the performance-gate skill on these components. Look for expensive render work, unstable props, large lists, and missing cleanup.

prompt_templates.title: Scale Scenario Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a performance-gate review as if this feature handles 10,000 and 1,000,000 records. Prioritize issues by scale impact and give warning questions for reviewers.

output_examples.input: Review a user list endpoint that returns all users without pagination.
output_examples.output: Performance gate warning. The endpoint can return too much data as the user table grows. Add pagination and select only required fields.

output_examples.input: Review a component that sorts a large array during every render.
output_examples.output: Performance gate warning. Sorting during render repeats work on every state change. Cache the sorted list when the input changes.

output_examples.input: Review an endpoint that loads users with related posts in one indexed query.
output_examples.output: Performance gate passed. The data access pattern avoids obvious N+1 queries and uses a bounded query shape.

best_practices: Run the gate after functional correctness is clear and before final review.
Include relevant files, data sizes, and expected traffic when asking for review.
Treat warnings as prompts for design discussion, not automatic rejection.
anti_patterns: Using the gate as a replacement for benchmarks or profiling tools.
Optimizing tiny datasets or rare admin paths without user impact.
Ignoring warnings about unbounded queries, large payloads, or repeated render work.

faq.question: Does this skill run performance tests?
faq.answer: No. It reviews code and design patterns for likely performance risks.

faq.question: Can it find N+1 queries?
faq.answer: It can flag likely N+1 patterns when related query code is provided.

faq.question: Does it block a release?
faq.answer: No. The skill is designed to produce pass or warning outcomes.

faq.question: Is it useful for frontend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks render cost, memoization, large lists, lazy loading, and cleanup patterns.

faq.question: Is it useful for backend code?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks query count, pagination, selected fields, indexing, and payload size.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide changed files, expected data volume, traffic assumptions, and any relevant database or component context.
